thing-type.label: Xbox Console
thing-type.description: An Xbox gaming console controlled via SmartGlass and the Xbox Live cloud (remote power, app launch, status, storage).

parameter.name: host
parameter.label: IP Address / Hostname
parameter.description: The IP address or hostname of the Xbox console (leave empty for broadcast).

parameter.name: authenticationUrl
parameter.label: Login QR-Code
parameter.description: Scan this QR code with your phone to sign in with your Microsoft account. Appears automatically a few seconds after creating the Thing. The code is pre-filled — just confirm the sign-in on your phone.

parameter.name: userCode
parameter.label: Verification Code
parameter.description: If you can't scan: open microsoft.com/link on any device and enter this code. Filled automatically.

parameter.name: clientId
parameter.label: Client ID (optional)
parameter.description: Leave empty to use the built-in Xbox app sign-in (recommended — no Azure account needed). Only set this if you want to use your own registered Microsoft/Azure application ID.

parameter.name: refreshToken
parameter.label: Refresh Token
parameter.description: Microsoft OAuth Refresh Token. Wird nach erfolgreichem QR-Login automatisch gefuellt — normalerweise musst du hier nichts eintragen. Danach geht das Thing automatisch ONLINE.

parameter.name: pollingInterval
parameter.label: Polling Interval
parameter.description: How often (in seconds) the console status is polled. Default 600, minimum 5.

parameter.name: appList
parameter.label: App Launch List
parameter.description: The apps shown in the "Launch App" channel, one entry per line as "Name=StoreProductId". Pre-filled with sensible defaults — simply add or remove a line to add/remove an app. The name is shown in the dropdown; the Store Product ID is what gets launched. Find an app's ID in its Microsoft Store link: apps.microsoft.com/detail/<ID>.

channel-type.label: Power
channel-type.description: Power state of the Xbox console

channel-type.label: Launch App
channel-type.description: Launch a specific application. The options come from the Thing's "App Launch List" config (pre-filled with defaults; the entries below are only the fallback). Sending a raw Store Product ID as a command also works.

channel-type.label: Current Title
channel-type.description: The name of the currently running application or game

channel-type.label: Cover Art
channel-type.description: Cover art of the currently running application or game

channel-type.label: Storage Free
channel-type.description: Free space on the internal storage of the console

channel-type.label: Storage Total
channel-type.description: Total size of the internal storage of the console

channel-type.label: Storage Used
channel-type.description: Used storage as a percentage of total capacity
